description_long: This is a two-story residential house with a rectangular footprint and a steeply pitched gable roof covered in reddish-brown ceramic tiles. The exterior walls are finished in a light beige stucco with subtle textural variation and horizontal trim bands separating the stories. The front facade features a symmetrical arrangement of six-paned windows and a central entry door accessed by a small set of steps. Two brick chimneys rise from the roof ridge, adding to the traditional European architectural character. The house shows some weathering and discoloration on the lower walls, suggesting age and exposure to the elements.
description: A two-story beige stucco house with a red-tiled gable roof, symmetrical windows, and a central entry door with steps.
description_view_0: Side view showing two vertically aligned windows per floor, beige stucco walls, and the sloped red-tiled roof with a chimney visible.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the main entry door with steps, four windows on each floor in symmetrical rows, and both chimneys on the roof.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with two vertically aligned windows per floor, visible weathering on the lower wall, and the sloped roof with a chimney.
description_view_3: Rear view showing a plain beige wall with no windows or doors, and the full expanse of the red-tiled gable roof with two chimneys.
attribute_entryway_design: Central single door with rectangular steps, no porch or columns, simple trim.
